name: Jeanette MacDonald
abstract: Jeanette Anna MacDonald (June 18, 1903 – January 14, 1965) was an American soprano and actress best remembered for her musical films of the 1930s with Maurice Chevalier (The Love Parade, Love Me Tonight, The Merry Widow and One Hour with You) and Nelson Eddy (Naughty Marietta, Rose-Marie, and Maytime). During the 1930s and 1940s she star…

about_quotes.speaker: Tom McIntosh
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee mentions Jeanette MacDonald only as part of a comparison to a school play ('black remake of Nelson Eddy and Jeanette MacDonald'), indicating no personal relationship.
about_quotes.doc_title: Tom McIntosh — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jeff Riddick
about_quotes.evidence: You would think that you were looking at Jeanette MacDonald. You know, she looked very much like her.

about_quotes.speaker: Ronnie Zito
about_quotes.evidence: And like Jeanette MacDonald, all those — beautiful music. It was beautiful you know. But that’s what they did.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ronnie Zito interviewed by Monk Rowe & Rick Montalbano, via Zoom, March 9, 2022

about_quotes.speaker: Skitch Henderson
about_quotes.evidence: Nelson Eddy and Jeannette MacDonald. That was my first training
about_quotes.doc_title: Skitch Henderson interviewed by Monk Rowe, New Milford, Connecticut, October 31, 1998
